claim: Pi is a minimal coding agent SDK with core packages (agent-core, SDK, AI abstraction, terminal UI) leveraged by OpenClaw; design principle: make systems easy for agents rather than fighting agent limitations
actionable: When building agent systems, optimize for agent usability: expose data via simple CLIs, design schemas agents understand naturally, avoid complex abstractions that confuse LLMs
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Core agent definition: LLM calling tools in a loop with goals, context, and tool calls/results; everything beyond this is implementation detail tailored to specific use cases
actionable: Build agents starting with this core pattern: define tools, set context/goals, run LLM in loop. Avoid over-engineering beyond this fundamental architecture
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Extension API enables agents to interact with UI (context UI select, dropdowns); agents can reason across backend systems and present interface elements dynamically
actionable: Use extension APIs to expose UI interactions to agents; enables agents to guide users through complex workflows rather than just returning raw data
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Coding agents discover capabilities by exploring available tools; e.g., agent used FFmpeg without explicit training because FFmpeg was available as a tool and agent figured out its relevance
actionable: Expose useful tools to agents and trust them to discover relevant tool combinations; agents are excellent at tool composition without explicit instruction
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Real-world B2B sales pipeline: route incoming RFP emails to customer-specific agents, use CLIs to expose CRM/ERP data, agents draft responses, humans edit and send
actionable: Build email-driven agent workflows: route messages to session-aware agents, use CLI tools for data access, output drafts for human approval; keeps humans in control while automating analysis
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Agent sessions preserve conversation history and context; using sessions allows agents to reference previous interactions and maintain coherent reasoning across multiple tasks
actionable: Implement agent sessions for multi-turn workflows; this enables agents to remember context and make decisions based on previous interactions
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Agent hooks (before/after tool calls) enable policy enforcement; e.g., validate permission before updating contact without explicitly teaching model about authorization checks
actionable: Use tool call hooks to enforce policies (role-based access, audit logging) without relying on model to understand security requirements
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product

claim: Multi-channel environment (OpenClaw): Pi provides core mechanics (sessions, events, tools) while OpenClaw adds plugin mechanism for routing, orchestration, gateway support across channels
actionable: Separate core agent mechanics (Pi) from deployment orchestration (OpenClaw); allows reusing Pi for simple single-channel agents or scaling to multi-channel with OpenClaw
source_title: A Piece of Pi: Embedding The OpenClaw Coding Agent In Your Product
